steps.title: Start with slope intercept form
steps.content:
A line with slope $m$ can be written as $y=mx+b$. Since the slope is $-\frac{1}{3}$, line $u$ has the form
$$y=-\frac{x}{3}+b$$

steps.title: Find the y intercept
steps.content:
Substitute the point $(9,5)$ and solve for $b$.
$$5=-\frac{9}{3}+b$$
$$5=-3+b$$
$$b=8$$
So the equation is $y=-\frac{x}{3}+8$. The answer is D.
